results.title: Nvidia Q3 FY2026 Results: Revenue $35.1 Billion, Data Center Revenue $30.8 Billion
results.snippet: Nvidia reported Q3 FY2026 revenue of $35.1 billion, up 94% year-over-year and 17% sequentially. Data Center revenue reached $30.8 billion (+112% YoY), reflecting high shipments of Blackwell B200 and H200 GPUs to cloud providers and enterprises. Gaming revenue was $3.3 billion (+15% YoY). Gross margin was 74.6%. GAAP EPS was $0.78.

results.title: Nvidia Blackwell GPU Ramp: Hyperscalers Order H200 and B200 for AI Infrastructure Build-Out
results.snippet: Nvidia's Blackwell B200 and GB200 GPUs are in high demand from cloud hyperscalers including Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud, Amazon AWS, and Meta. CEO Jensen Huang said production is being ramped at TSMC through CoWoS advanced packaging. Nvidia supplies the majority of GPU accelerators used in large AI training and inference workloads globally.

results.title: Nvidia CUDA Platform Has Over 5 Million Registered Developers Globally
results.snippet: Nvidia's CUDA parallel computing platform has over 5 million registered developers globally. Nvidia's NIM microservices and AI Enterprise software stack generate recurring subscription revenue estimated at $2.5 billion annually. The company's networking business (InfiniBand, Ethernet switches) contributed $3.1 billion in revenue in Q3 FY2026.

results.title: Nvidia Named Key Supplier in $500 Billion Stargate US AI Infrastructure Project on January 22, 2026
results.snippet: Nvidia was named a key technology supplier in the Stargate AI infrastructure project on January 22, 2026. The $500 billion initiative, backed by SoftBank, OpenAI, and Oracle, will use Nvidia GPUs for large-scale AI compute. Nvidia's next-generation GB300 GPU is targeted for volume shipments in H2 2026, with performance-per-watt improvements over the current Blackwell architecture.
